stories.title: Alabama Opens Title Run at Tuscaloosa Regional — No. 1 Seed's 21-Year Hosting Streak Meets Friday

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Alabama is 49-7. Second-best ERA in college softball at 1.60. 22 shutouts this season.

The No. 1 seed opens the Tuscaloosa Regional Friday vs. USC Upstate at Rhoads Stadium.

This is the most dominant team in the country, and they know it. 🥎 #RollTide #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Alabama has hosted 21 straight NCAA regionals. That's not just consistency — that's a dynasty standard.

No. 1 seed. 49-7 record. The Tide's title window doesn't get much bigger than 2026.

Tuscaloosa Regional starts Friday. 🥎 #RollTide #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Alabama Opens Title Run at Tuscaloosa Regional — The No. 1 Seed's 21-Year Hosting Streak Continues Friday

stories.title: Oklahoma Goes for Nine — Sooners Bring .389 Offense and Dynasty History to Norman Regional

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma won 4 straight national titles (2021–2024). They want No. 9 overall.

The Sooners bring a .389 batting average — best in the nation — and 174 HRs into the Norman Regional. First pitch Friday vs. Binghamton.

The dynasty isn't done. 🥎 #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma is hosting their 15th straight regional. 15 years showing up in May.

Now they're at Love's Field with Michigan, Kansas, and Binghamton standing between them and a super regional.

They've done it 8 times before. They know how to win in May. 🥎 #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Oklahoma Goes for Nine: Sooners Launch Norman Regional Bid for Fifth Title in Six Years

stories.title: Nebraska's Lincoln Regional — Big Ten Champs Host for First Time Since 2013

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Nebraska won its 11th Big Ten championship and earned the No. 4 national seed.

Friday they host a regional for the first time since 2013. Opener vs. South Dakota at 5:30 PM ET on ESPN+.

The Lincoln Regional is back, Husker fans. 🌽 #Huskers #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Nebraska's Long-Awaited Return: Big Ten Champs Host Lincoln Regional for First Time Since 2013

stories.title: Megan Grant's Final Run — 38 HRs and a Shot at a Championship Finale for UCLA

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant hit 38 home runs this season. All-time NCAA record.

She's a senior. This L.A. Regional could be her last games in a UCLA uniform. No. 8 seed at Easton Stadium, first pitch Friday.

She broke the record. Now can she win a title? 🥎 #UCLA #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Megan Grant's Final Run: 38 HRs and a Shot at a Championship Finale at the L.A. Regional

stories.title: Virginia Tech in Baton Rouge — The Bracket's Best Upset Pick Targets No. 16 LSU

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Virginia Tech went 46-10. The NCAA said they can't host. So the Hokies head to Baton Rouge as No. 2 seed behind No. 16 LSU.

First game vs. South Alabama, Friday 4 PM ET on ESPN+.

Upset alert: VT is the top dark horse to escape this regional. 🥎 #Hokies #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Virginia Tech in Baton Rouge: Why the Hokies Are the Bracket's Best Upset Pick

stories.title: 2026 Softball Regionals TV Guide — Full Schedule, Networks, and Streaming Info

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
2026 NCAA Softball Regionals start Friday. Where to watch:

🟠 Alabama vs. USC Upstate — 1 PM ET, SEC Network
🟠 Oklahoma vs. Binghamton — 2:30 PM ET
🟠 VT vs. South Alabama — 4 PM ET, ESPN+
🟠 Nebraska vs. South Dakota — 5:30 PM ET, ESPN+

All 16 sites. 48 hours. 🥎 #CollegeSoftball

research.facts:
TV Guide
**Source:** ESPN/NCAA schedule
- All 16 regionals run May 15–17 (HIGH)
- Networks: ESPN, ESPN2, ESPNU, ESPN+, SEC Network, Big Ten Network (HIGH)
- WCWS Super Regionals: May 22–24 (HIGH)
- Women's College World Series: May 28–June 4/5 at Devon Park, Oklahoma City (HIGH)
- Key regional game times: Alabama vs. USC Upstate (1 PM ET Fri, SEC Network), Nebraska vs. South Dakota (5:30 PM ET Fri, ESPN+), Oklahoma vs. Binghamton (2:30 PM ET Fri), VT vs. South Alabama (4 PM ET Fri, ESPN+), Texas Tech vs. Marist (3:30 PM ET Fri, ESPN+) (HIGH)
